eval_prompt: 我们的 Aether 集群最近不太稳定，帮我做一份完整的健康检查。Nomad 地址 http://192.168.69.70:4646，Consul 在 http://192.168.69.70:8500。需要看所有节点状态、运行中的 Jobs、有没有失败的 allocation、Consul 服务健康情况。特别关注 heavy 节点的负载分布是否均匀。如果有问题请给出处理建议。

criterion_1_nomad_node_status.description: 必须查询并展示 Nomad 节点状态（heavy + light 节点数量和 ready/down 状态）

output_a.notes: Full table with all 8 nodes showing IP, status (all ready), NodeClass, scheduling eligibility, drain status, drivers. Separate hardware specs table for heavy nodes with CPU/memory/disk details.

output_b.notes: Full table with all 8 nodes showing IP, type, status (all ready), CPU, memory, disk, Nomad version, Docker version. Also lists 3 infra-server Consul nodes separately.

criterion_2_failed_allocations.description: 必须查询并展示失败的 allocation 信息，包括关联的 Job 名称和所在节点

output_a.notes: Three detailed sections: celery (failed, heavy-1, 39 restarts, Docker driver failure with error), celery-beat (failed, heavy-1, 83 restarts, same driver failure), and api (running but unhealthy). Critically, identified root cause from API stderr logs: database 'psych_supervision' does not exist. Full failure chain traced.

output_b.notes: Two sections for celery and celery-beat with error details, node, and driver failure messages. Notes psych-ai-dev-api as running but unhealthy from Consul side. Also includes deployment failure message (progress deadline). However, does NOT identify the actual root cause (missing database) from application logs.

criterion_3_nomad_and_consul_data.description: 必须同时查询 Nomad 和 Consul 两个数据源，提供完整的集群健康视图

output_a.notes: Nomad data: nodes, jobs with node placement, allocations with logs. Consul data: health check counts (48 passing, 1 critical), full service-by-node table, critical check details. Root cause analysis correlates data from both sources.

output_b.notes: Nomad data: nodes, jobs with historical failure counts, allocations. Consul data: critical/warning/passing breakdown, detailed service list with traefik routing tags. Additionally surfaces application-level health data from Consul output (wecom-relay tokenValid, kairos-prod component status).

criterion_4_structured_format.description: 输出应为结构化格式，包含分区信息

output_a.notes: 8 well-organized sections with summary tables at top and bottom. Logical flow: overview -> node status -> jobs -> failures -> Consul -> load analysis -> root cause -> recommendations. Consistent use of Markdown tables throughout.

output_b.notes: 9 sections with clear hierarchy. Includes summary assessment table at top and priority table at end. Logical flow: overview -> nodes -> jobs -> failures -> Consul -> load -> recommendations -> service overview -> priorities. Consistent formatting.

criterion_5_anomaly_highlighting.description: 对异常项应有明显标注或高亮提示

output_a.notes: Uses [CRITICAL] and [WARNING] tags on section headers. FAIL/WARN/PASS status indicators in summary tables. Bold text for critical metrics (100.5% MEM over-allocation). ASCII bar chart for visual load comparison. P0/P1/P2 priority labels on recommendations.

output_b.notes: Uses bold text and '需要关注'/'严重不均衡' markers. ASCII bar chart for load visualization. Blockquote annotations for important notes. P0-P3 priority table. Less visually distinctive anomaly markers compared to A (no explicit FAIL/WARN/PASS status per item in summary).

output_a.content_notes: Deep root cause analysis: identified missing 'psych_supervision' database from API stderr logs and traced the full failure chain (DB missing -> API crash -> Consul critical -> celery/celery-beat crash -> Docker filesystem residue). Calculated per-node resource utilization with exact percentages, discovered memory over-allocation on heavy-1 (3936/3915 MB = 100.5%). Provided specific migration candidates (openstock-dev, kairos-dev, silknode-web) with projected post-migration resource distribution. Included HCL examples for spread stanza.
output_a.structure_notes: Excellent organization with 8 sections flowing logically from assessment to analysis to actionable recommendations. Summary table at beginning and detailed checklist at end provide easy navigation. P0/P1/P2 prioritization clearly separates urgency levels.

output_b.content_notes: Good breadth of findings: surfaced wecom-relay token expiration and kairos-prod component failures (wecomApi: false, relayClient: false) that A missed. Included historical failure counts per job and Consul service routing tags. However, missed the critical root cause identification (missing database) — attributed the API unhealthiness to 'application process not listening on port' without investigating logs. Did not calculate resource utilization percentages or identify memory over-allocation.
output_b.structure_notes: Well organized with 9 sections and consistent formatting. Priority table at the end is useful. Consul service overview with routing tags adds value. Slightly less cohesive flow than A — the root cause analysis is not centralized but distributed across sections.

part2_blind_comparison.winner_justification: Output A wins decisively on diagnostic depth. The identification of the missing 'psych_supervision' database as root cause — from actual application stderr logs — is the single most valuable finding in either report and directly enables resolution. A's resource utilization calculations revealing memory over-allocation (100.5%) on heavy-1 provide a systemic explanation for cluster instability. The projected post-migration resource distribution demonstrates actionable planning depth. While B surfaces additional surface-level issues (wecom-relay token, kairos-prod components) that add value, these are secondary findings compared to A's root cause analysis and quantitative resource assessment.
notable_differences: A identified root cause (missing psych_supervision database) from API stderr logs; B did not investigate application logs
A calculated exact resource utilization percentages and discovered memory over-allocation (100.5%) on heavy-1; B did not perform resource math
A provided specific migration candidates with projected post-migration resource distribution; B suggested migration without projections
B found wecom-relay token expiration and kairos-prod component failures that A missed
B included historical failure counts per job (e.g., nexus-api-dev: 7 failures) that A did not mention
B included Consul service routing tags (traefik URLs) that A did not include
